input.content: You are producing a structured action for an agentic workflow. Return only valid JSON matching the provided schema. Do not include explanations outside the JSON.

# Document

Based on the following workflow state and execution context, determine the next structured action. Use exact values from the context for all fields.

<source>
ONBOARDING AUTOMATION AGENT — EXECUTION TRACE LOG
====================================================
Session ID: onb-agent-20240911-3f7a
Workspace: ghostbusters-hq.slack.com
Agent Version: 2.14.0-rc3
Started: 2024-09-11T14:22:01Z

================================================================================
WORKFLOW DEFINITION
================================================================================

Workflow Name: new_hire_onboarding_v3
Description: Automated onboarding sequence that authenticates against the Slack
  workspace, resolves the new hire's Slack user ID, retrieves their full profile
  (including locale, timezone, admin status, and email), then provisions channels,
  sends welcome messages, and logs the onboarding event to the HR system.

Steps Overview:
  Step 1 — Validate authentication token and scopes
  Step 2 — Resolve target user ID via users.list search
  Step 3 — Invoke users.info to retrieve full user profile
  Step 4 — Provision default channels and send welcome DM
  Step 5 — Log onboarding record to HR system webhook

Current workflow_step_number: 3

Constraints:
  - All API calls must respect published rate limits.
  - The agent must request include_locale = true for every profile fetch so
    downstream localization can format messages correctly.
  - Email access requires both users:read and users:read.email scopes.
  - If a user_not_found error is returned, the workflow must halt and alert HR.

================================================================================
STEP 1 — AUTHENTICATION VALIDATION (COMPLETED)
================================================================================

[2024-09-11T14:22:02Z] ACTION: validate_token
  Token pattern: xxxx-xxxxxxxxx-xxxx
  Method: auth.test via GET request
  Endpoint: https://slack.com/api/auth.test

[2024-09-11T14:22:02Z] REQUEST HEADERS:
  Authorization: Bearer xxxx-xxxxxxxxx-xxxx
  Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

[2024-09-11T14:22:03Z] RESPONSE (200 OK):
  {
    "ok": true,
    "url": "https://ghostbusters-hq.slack.com/",
    "team": "Ghostbusters HQ",
    "user": "onboarding-bot",
    "team_id": "T012AB3C4",
    "user_id": "U0BOTID99",
    "bot_id": "B0BOTID99",
    "is_enterprise_install": false
  }

[2024-09-11T14:22:03Z] SCOPE CHECK:
  Installed scopes for bot_token:
    - users:read          ✓ (is_required: true for users.info)
    - users:read.email    ✓ (needed for email field)
    - channels:read       ✓
    - channels:manage     ✓
    - chat:write          ✓
    - im:write            ✓
    - groups:read         ✓

  Note on scope requirements for users.info:
    Bot token: users:read — this scope is required (is_required: true) for
      the users.info method.
    User token: users:read — this scope is also required (is_required: true)
      when calling users.info on behalf of a user. Since this workflow uses a
      bot token, the bot_token scope takes precedence.

  The scope_name "users:read" must be present for the token_type being used.
  For bot_token, users:read is_required = true.
  For user_token, users:read is_required = true.

  IMPORTANT — Apps created after January 4th, 2017 must request both users:read
  and users:read.email OAuth permission scopes simultaneously when using the
  OAuth app installation flow to enable access to the email field of user objects
  returned by the users.info method. The users:read scope alone is no longer
  sufficient to access email addresses in user objects.

[2024-09-11T14:22:03Z] RESULT: Authentication validated. Token is active,
  scopes are sufficient. Proceeding to Step 2.

================================================================================
STEP 2 — USER LOOKUP VIA users.list (COMPLETED)
================================================================================

[2024-09-11T14:22:04Z] ACTION: invoke_api_method
  Target: users.list
  HTTP: GET request
  Rate limit: Tier 2 (20+ per minute) — note this differs from users.info
  Purpose: Search workspace members for the new hire matching HR record
           email = spengler@ghostbusters.example.com

[2024-09-11T14:22:04Z] REQUEST:
  GET https://slack.com/api/users.list?limit=200
  Authorization: Bearer xxxx-xxxxxxxxx-xxxx

[2024-09-11T14:22:05Z] RESPONSE (200 OK) — truncated for brevity:
  {
    "ok": true,
    "members": [
      {
        "id": "U0ADMIN01",
        "team_id": "T012AB3C4",
        "name": "venkman",
        "real_name": "Peter Venkman",
        "is_admin": false,
        "is_owner": true,
        "deleted": false,
        "profile": {
          "email": "venkman@ghostbusters.example.com",
          "display_name": "venkman"
        }
      },
      {
        "id": "W012A3CDE",
        "team_id": "T012AB3C4",
        "name": "spengler",
        "real_name": "Egon Spengler",
        "is_admin": true,
        "is_owner": false,
        "is_bot": false,
        "is_app_user": false,
        "deleted": false,
        "color": "9f69e7",
        "updated": 1502138686,
        "has_2fa": false,
        "profile": {
          "email": "spengler@ghostbusters.example.com",
          "display_name": "spengler",
          "status_text": "Print is dead",
          "avatar_hash": "ge3b51ca72de"
        }
      },
      {
        "id": "U0STANZ03",
        "team_id": "T012AB3C4",
        "name": "stantz",
        "real_name": "Ray Stantz",
        "is_admin": false,
        "is_owner": false,
        "deleted": false,
        "profile": {
          "email": "stantz@ghostbusters.example.com",
          "display_name": "stantz"
        }
      }
    ],
    "response_metadata": {
      "next_cursor": ""
    }
  }

[2024-09-11T14:22:05Z] MATCH FOUND:
  Resolved user ID W012A3CDE for email spengler@ghostbusters.example.com
  Preliminary profile data captured (partial — users.list does not return
  full profile fields such as timezone, locale, or high-resolution images).

  Partial user object from users.list:
    user_id: W012A3CDE
    team_id: T012AB3C4
    name: spengler
    real_name: Egon Spengler
    color_hex: 9f69e7
    is_admin: true
    is_owner: false
    is_bot: false
    is_app_user: false
    has_2fa: false
    deleted: false
    updated_timestamp: 1502138686

  Note: The updated_timestamp 1502138686 corresponds to 2017-08-07T22:04:46Z.
  The color_hex value 9f69e7 is system-assigned and used for sidebar display.

[2024-09-11T14:22:05Z] RESULT: Target user resolved. Proceeding to Step 3.

================================================================================
STEP 3 — RETRIEVE FULL USER PROFILE VIA users.info (PENDING)
================================================================================

[2024-09-11T14:22:06Z] PLANNING NEXT ACTION...

The agent has completed authentication validation (step 1) and resolved the
target user ID W012A3CDE from a prior users.list call (step 2). The next action
is to invoke the users.info API method via GET request with include_locale set
to true in order to retrieve the full user profile including timezone, admin
status, bot flag, and email for downstream processing in the onboarding
automation workflow.

--- API Reference Snapshot: users.info ---

Target method: users.info
Action type: invoke_api_method
HTTP verb: get_request
Endpoint: GET https://slack.com/api/users.info

Rate Limits:
  This method is classified as Tier 4: 100+ per minute.
  The rate_limit_tier is 4 and the rate_limit_description is
  "Tier 4: 100+ per minute". This is more generous than users.list (Tier 2)
  or conversations.list (Tier 3). Tier 1 methods are limited to 1+ per minute,
  Tier 2 to 20+ per minute, Tier 3 to 50+ per minute.

Content Types Accepted:
  The method accepts the following content_types:
    - application/x-www-form-urlencoded
    - application/json

Required Arguments:
  token (string) — Required. Authentication token bearing required scopes.
    Tokens should be passed as an HTTP Authorization header or alternatively
    as a POST parameter. Example value: xxxx-xxxxxxxxx-xxxx
  user (string) — Required. The Slack user ID to look up.
    For this invocation: W012A3CDE

Optional Arguments:
  include_locale (boolean) — Optional. Set this to true to receive the locale
    for this user. Defaults to false. The include_locale_default is false, but
    in this workflow we explicitly set include_locale to true because the
    onboarding messages must be localized.

Scopes Required:
  Bot token: users:read (scope_name: "users:read", token_type: "bot_token")
  User token: users:read (scope_name: "users:read", token_type: "user_token")

--- Expected Response Shape ---

When the call succeeds, the response JSON will contain an "ok": true field
and a "user" object. The profile sub-object within the user object contains
numerous fields. Not all fields are guaranteed to be present; many depend on
what the workspace member has configured.

Expected profile fields and their anticipated values for user W012A3CDE:

  field_name: real_name
    field_value: "Egon Spengler"
    field_source: default_profile
    is_guaranteed: false (users may not set a real name)

  field_name: display_name
    field_value: "spengler"
    field_source: default_profile
    is_guaranteed: false (users may leave display_name blank)

  field_name: email
    field_value: "spengler@ghostbusters.example.com"
    field_source: oauth_scope (requires users:read.email scope)
    is_guaranteed: false (only present if the app has the email scope)

  field_name: status_text
    field_value: "Print is dead"
    field_source: default_profile
    is_guaranteed: false (users may clear their status at any time)

  field_name: image_original
    field_value: "https://.../avatar/e3b51ca72dee4ef87916ae2b9240df50.jpg"
    field_source: default_profile
    is_guaranteed: true (Slack always generates at least one avatar URL)

  field_name: avatar_hash
    field_value: "ge3b51ca72de"
    field_source: system_generated
    is_guaranteed: false (may be absent for users with default avatars,
      though typically present once a custom photo is uploaded)

Additional fields that may appear in the user object but are not primary
targets for this workflow:
  - tz (timezone string, e.g. "America/Los_Angeles")
  - tz_label (e.g. "Pacific Daylight Time")
  - tz_offset (integer, e.g. -25200 which is UTC-7 in seconds)
  - is_restricted, is_ultra_restricted (guest account flags)
  - is_stranger (see shared channel note below)
  - locale (e.g. "en-US", only present when include_locale is true)

Example user object (anticipated full response for W012A3CDE):
  {
    "user_id": "W012A3CDE",
    "team_id": "T012AB3C4",
    "name": "spengler",
    "real_name": "Egon Spengler",
    "timezone": "America/Los_Angeles",
    "tz_offset": -25200,
    "is_admin": true,
    "is_owner": false,
    "is_bot": false,
    "is_app_user": false,
    "has_2fa": false,
    "deleted": false,
    "updated_timestamp": 1502138686,
    "color_hex": "9f69e7"
  }

--- Shared Channel Note ---

When looking up a user belonging to a foreign workspace party to a shared
channel, the response will include an is_stranger boolean attribute indicating
the user belongs to an external workspace connected through Slack Connect. In
this workflow, user W012A3CDE is a local workspace member, so is_stranger
should not appear or will be false.

--- Known Errors ---

The following errors may be returned by users.info. The agent must handle
each according to its category and transience:

  error_code: user_not_found
    error_category: resource_not_found
    is_transient: false
    description_summary: The user ID provided does not correspond to any user
      in the workspace.
    Agent action: Halt workflow, alert HR team via #onboarding-alerts channel.

  error_code: account_inactive
    error_category: authentication_failure
    is_transient: false
    description_summary: Authentication token is for a deleted user or workspace
      when using a bot token.
    Agent action: Halt workflow, re-authenticate, then retry once.

  error_code: access_denied
    error_category: authorization_failure
    is_transient: false
    description_summary: Access to a resource specified in the request is denied.
    Agent action: Halt workflow, check scope configuration.

  error_code: fatal_error
    error_category: server_error
    is_transient: true
    description_summary: The server could not complete the operation without
      encountering a catastrophic error. Some aspect of the operation may have
      succeeded before the error was raised.
    Agent action: Retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff (since
      is_transient is true).

  error_code: deprecated_endpoint
    error_category: deprecated_endpoint
    is_transient: false
    description_summary: The endpoint has been deprecated and should no longer
      be called.
    Agent action: Halt workflow, notify engineering to update integration.

  Other possible errors not expected in this flow:
    - not_authed (no token provided)
    - invalid_auth (token is invalid)
    - missing_scope (required scope not granted)
    - ratelimited (too many requests; respect Retry-After header)
    - request_timeout (transient, retry)

--- Email Scope Note ---

Apps created after January 4th, 2017 must request both users:read and
users:read.email OAuth permission scopes simultaneously when using the OAuth
app installation flow to enable access to the email field of user objects
returned by the users.info method. The users:read scope alone is no longer
sufficient to access email addresses in user objects. This workflow's bot token
was installed with both scopes, so the email field should be present in the
response.

# User Request

Given the completed authentication and user lookup steps in an onboarding automation workflow, produce the next structured action to invoke the Slack users.info API method with all required parameters and expected response metadata.

# Response Notes

- Copy exact source strings for names, dates, URLs, identifiers, titles, counts, and quoted text.
- Do not expand acronyms, add parentheticals, rephrase, or normalize titles unless explicitly told to.
- Preserve casing and punctuation for copied values.
- Convert numerical fields into plain numeric values without units.
- For enum fields, choose the schema value that best matches the source characterization.
- Preserve source order for result arrays.
- Do NOT extract more or fewer items than specified — array lengths are strictly validated.
- All values needed are stated in the source above. The challenge is mapping them into the correct schema structure.
- Extract exactly 2 scopes (no more, no fewer).
- Extract exactly 6 expected profile fields (no more, no fewer).
- Extract exactly 5 known errors (no more, no fewer).
- Extract exactly 2 content types (no more, no fewer).
- Output must be valid JSON (parseable by a standard JSON parser).
- Use correct JSON types: integers as numbers, booleans as true/false, arrays as [].

# Question

Produce valid JSON matching the schema for this agent action.

# Schema

# Schema Documentation

This document describes the expected structure for an agent action configuration object. The top-level object contains a single required property called **agent_action**, and no additional properties are allowed.

---

## Agent Action

The **agent_action** is an object that defines all aspects of an API-related action an agent can perform. It contains the following required fields, with no additional properties permitted:

### Core Action Properties

- **action_type**: A string that must be one of the following values: "invoke_api_method", "retry_with_backoff", "escalate_to_human", "parse_response", or "update_state". This indicates what kind of action the agent is performing.

- **target_method**: A string representing the API method to call. It must follow the pattern of a lowercase word, followed by a dot, followed by another lowercase word (e.g., "users.info").

- **http_verb**: A string indicating the HTTP method to use, restricted to one of: "get_request", "post_request", "put_request", or "delete_request".

- **rate_limit_tier**: An integer representing the rate limit tier that applies to this API method.

- **rate_limit_description**: A string providing a human-readable description of the rate limiting behavior.

### Arguments

- **required_arguments**: An object containing the mandatory parameters for the API call. It must include exactly two fields and no others:
  - **token**: A string representing the authentication token.
  - **user**: A string representing a user identifier, which must start with the letter "W" followed by one or more uppercase alphanumeric characters (e.g., "W012A3CDE").

- **optional_arguments**: An object containing optional parameters for the API call. It must include exactly two fields and no others:
  - **include_locale**: A boolean indicating whether to include locale information.
  - **include_locale_default**: A boolean indicating whether to include default locale information.

### Scopes

- **scopes**: An array containing exactly 2 scope entries. Each scope entry is an object with the following required fields (no additional properties allowed):
  - **scope_name**: A string representing the OAuth scope name, which must consist only of lowercase letters, colons, dots, and underscores (e.g., "users:read").
  - **token_type**: A string that must be either "bot_token" or "user_token".
  - **is_required**: A boolean indicating whether this scope is mandatory.

### Expected Profile Fields

- **expected_profile_fields**: An array containing exactly 6 user profile detail entries. Each entry is an object with the following required fields (no additional properties allowed):
  - **field_name**: A string identifying the profile field (e.g., "email", "display_name").
  - **field_value**: Either a string containing the field's value, or null if no value is available.
  - **field_source**: A string indicating where the field value originates, which must be one of: "default_profile", "custom_profile", "system_generated", or "oauth_scope".
  - **is_guaranteed**: A boolean indicating whether this field is guaranteed to be present in the response.

### Example User Object

- **example_user_object**: An object representing a sample user record returned by the API. It must contain exactly the following required fields (no additional properties allowed):
  - **user_id**: A string starting with "W" followed by uppercase alphanumeric characters.
  - **team_id**: A string starting with "T" followed by uppercase alphanumeric characters.
  - **name**: A string representing the user's username.
  - **real_name**: A string representing the user's full real name.
  - **timezone**: A string representing the user's timezone (e.g., "America/Los_Angeles").
  - **tz_offset**: An integer representing the timezone offset in seconds from UTC.
  - **is_admin**: A boolean indicating whether the user is a workspace admin.
  - **is_owner**: A boolean indicating whether the user is a workspace owner.
  - **is_bot**: A boolean indicating whether the user is a bot account.
  - **is_app_user**: A boolean indicating whether the user is an app user.
  - **has_2fa**: A boolean indicating whether the user has two-factor authentication enabled.
  - **deleted**: A boolean indicating whether the user account has been deleted.
  - **updated_timestamp**: An integer representing the Unix timestamp of the last profile update.
  - **color_hex**: Either a string representing a hex color code associated with the user, or null if none is assigned.

### Known Errors

- **known_errors**: An array containing exactly 5 error entries. Each error entry is an object with the following required fields (no additional properties allowed):
  - **error_code**: A string representing the specific error code returned by the API (e.g., "user_not_found").
  - **error_category**: A string classifying the error into one of the following categories: "authentication_failure", "authorization_failure", "resource_not_found", "rate_limit_exceeded", "server_error", "deprecated_endpoint", or "network_restriction".
  - **is_transient**: A boolean indicating whether the error is temporary and may resolve on retry.
  - **description_summary**: A string providing a brief human-readable explanation of the error.

### Additional Metadata

- **email_scope_note**: A string containing a note about the email-related OAuth scope requirements or behavior.

- **shared_channel_note**: Either a string containing a note about shared channel behavior, or null if not applicable.

- **content_types**: An array of exactly 2 strings representing the accepted or returned content types (e.g., "application/json", "application/x-www-form-urlencoded").

- **workflow_step_number**: An integer indicating the position of this action within a larger workflow sequence.

- **workflow_rationale**: A string explaining why this action is being performed at this step in the workflow.
